lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY guarded lifecycle for the eventstreams (Kafka DBaaS) WRITE endpoints. Primary target (the service's own GAP-write set): eventstreamsaddinstances (POST /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/add-instances). Also re-exercises the shared cluster sub-ops (restart/maintenance/security-group-rules/patch/resize/block-storage-resize) and eventstreamssetparametervalues (PUT /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/parameters — eventstreams-specific) so they record under eventstreams' own catalog keys (the gap analyzer matches path-only, but the engine records WRITE steps per (method,path,service)). Does NOT provision a real billable cluster: soft-captures an EXISTING cluster_id from the read-only list; when none exists the {cluster_id}/{instance_group_id}/{block_storage_group_id} tokens still normalize to the catalog '*' key so each endpoint is CALLED and RECORDED (a 4xx counts as covered). Every mutating step is optional + grouped + broad expect_status; DELETEs destructive:true. heavy:true gates behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY. Bodies best-effort from path/field semantics + data/api_bodies.json eventstreamscreatecluster shape — UNPROVEN, promote after a real 2xx. set-parameter-values needs a real parameter-group/parameter name/value (doc-sample used; likely 4xx, still records).

COVERAGE-ONLY read lifecycle for eventstreams' two id-bound GETs (READ-ONLY, never mutates). Lists /v1/engine-versions and soft-captures the id under the EXACT catalog path-param name (dbaas_engine_version_id) so the final probe-reads step exercises eventstreamslistengineversionproperties (/v1/engine-versions/{dbaas_engine_version_id}/properties). KNOWN-HARD: eventstreamsshowrequest (/v1/requests/{request_id}) — request_id is only returned by async write ops and is NOT exposed by any list/collection GET, so it cannot be harvested in a read-only run; it is called with a literal placeholder id so the endpoint is at least CALLED and RECORDED (a 404 counts as reached/covered per the engine). When the engine-versions collection is empty the {dbaas_engine_version_id} normalizes to the catalog '*' key so the properties endpoint is still CALLED. Every step optional + grouped + broad expect_status so an isolated failure never fails the run.
